definition: One still in training on the noble path, not yet fully complete.
notes: The project prefers trainee because it is direct and doctrinally precise enough without sounding stiff. One in training is acceptable in explanatory prose when the sentence needs a fuller phrase. The term should be heard as a formal path-classification: it refers to those who have entered noble training but have not yet reached the completion marked by the arahant. MN 117 is a useful control passage here: the trainee is eight-factored, while the completed side of the path opens into right knowledge and right release for the one beyond training. The OSF glossary also keeps sekha aligned with the technical trainee-versus-asekha distinction, supporting the current policy.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose where a fuller phrase reads more naturally
context_rules.notes: Allow this as a controlled alternate only when the context clearly supports it.

context_rules.context: When contrasting the noble disciple still on the path with the one beyond training
context_rules.notes: Keep the technical contrast with asekha explicit.

example_phrases.notes: Shows the unfinished but noble character of the stage.

authority_basis.scope: Named in notes as support for the current sekha policy.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'trainee' as the default rendering in the entry's governed translation contexts unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Yield to recorded context_rules, source-facing technical use, or other explicitly documented local reasons rather than rotating among alternates ad hoc.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents drift back toward 'beginner' or other unrecorded alternates.
